original: Chinese southern Song dynasty officially declares war on the northern Jin dynasty

While taking exile in Fuzhou, southern China, from advancing Mongol invaders, remnants of the Song Dynasty court hold coronation ceremony for young prince Zhao Shi, making him Emperor Duanzong of Song
ExplorerIbn Battutabegins his travels, leaving his home in Tangiers to travel to Mecca (gone 24 years)
Catharina de Medici and Duke of Alva discuss Calvinism
At 4:30 AM Dutch explorer Willem Barents leaves Novaya Zemlya on his third Arctic expedition to return to the Netherlands (dies on the return voyage)
Dutch mariner Jacques Le Maire sets sails from Texel, North Holland, to find a new route to the Pacific and the Spice Islands and the southern continent (Terra Australis)
1st breach-of-promise lawsuit: Rev Gerville Pooley, Virginia files against Cicely Jordan, he loses
Russia and Poland sign Peace treaty of Polianov
First compulsory education law in America is passed by Massachusetts
Battle of the Dunes: English and French forces defeat the Spanish near Dunkirk during Franco-Spanish War

French officer Simon-François Daumont de Saint-Lusson takes possession for France of Lake Huron and Superior "and all the other contiguous and adjacent countries, rivers, lakes and streams, here, both discovered and yet to be discovered", at assembly of fourteen Amerindian nations at Sault-Sainte-Marie
Battle at Schooneveld: Dutch admiral Michiel de Ruyter beats French and English fleet
Battle of Marengo (Alessandria):Napoleon Bonaparte's French army overcomes Austrian forces in Piedmont, Italy
EmperorNapoleonI's French Grande Armee defeats the Russian Army at the Battle of Friedland in Prussia (modern Russian Kaliningrad Oblast) ending War of the Fourth Coalition
King of Sennar, Badi VII, surrenders his throne and realm to General Ismail Pasha of the Ottoman Empire, bringing the 300-year-old Sudanese kingdom to an end
Hardhat diving suit patented by Leonard Norcross of  Dixfield, Maine
Sandpaper is patented by Isaac Fischer Jr. in Springfield, Vermont
First Henley Regatta held (it became the Henley Royal Regatta in 1851)
First Canadian Parliament opens in Kingston, Ontario
Belgian Liberal Party forms
California (Bear Flag) Republic proclaimed in Sonoma, declaring independence from Mexico
Third great fire of early San Francisco, starts in a bakery chimney
First train trip in South America, a train leaves Asuncion central station in Paraguay along the Asuncion - Encarnacion railway
Battle of 2nd Winchester, Virginia; Confederate force defeats Union army garrison and captures Winchester
US Union warship USS Kearsarge appears off Cherbourg
All-pro Cincinnati Red Stockings suffer 1st loss in 130 games
Trade unions are legalised in Canada
1st player to hit for cycle (George Hall, Philadelphia Athletics)
California Street Cable Car Railroad Co gets its franchise
Niger Convention signed in Paris by France and Great Britain agreeing to the partition of West Africa
Having been annexed to the USA on 12 August 1898, Hawaii is constituted as an organized territory
The Reichstag approves a second law that allows the expansion of the German navy
At the battle of Telissu, the Japanese rout the Russians and inflict heavy casualties
Dutch troops occupy Kuto Reh, Sumatra, killing all inhabitants
Pogrom against Jews in Bialystok, Polish Russia
Government of Transvaal sends home 50,000 Chinese day workers
Norway adopts female suffrage for middle class women only in parliamentary elections
Fourth German Navy Bill is passed authorising the financing of the building of another four major warships
Representatives of eight Allied nations hold an economic conference in Paris at which they discuss ways to cripple their enemies economic power during and after the war
First German air attack on England, Gotha bombers kill 162 civilians and injure 432 in East London
General Pershing and his HQ staff arrived in Paris during WW I
John Alcock and Arthur Brown leave Newfoundland in the first non-stop air crossing of the Atlantic

Orchestral version of Ralph Vaughn Williams’ “The Lark Ascending” premieres, with dedicatee Marie Hall as violinist and Adrian Boult conducting the British Symphony Orchestra.in the Queen's Hall, London
Charles Hoffner wins PGA golf tournament
Recording of first country music hit (Little Old Log Cabin in the Lane)
Test Cricket debuts of English players Herbert Sutcliffe and Maurice Tate in 1st Test England v South Africa at Edgbaston
WOKO-AM radio begins transmitting from Albany, NY
Prussia and Vatican sign Concord
VVGZ soccer team forms in Zwijndrecht
French steamer "St Philbert" overturns off St Nazaire, France, drowning 450 people
WOQ-AM in Kansas City Missouri goes off the air
Chaco War between Bolivia & Paraguay ends
Oranienburg Concentration Camp opens in Germany
American children's literature writer and illustration Dorothy Lathrop wins 1st Caldecott Medal for Illustration for her work "Animals of the Bible"
Chlorophyll patented by Benjamin Grushkin
German forces enter Paris during WWII
German U-47 sinks airship Balmoral
Estonia loses 11,000 inhabitants as a consequence of mass deportations into Siberia
1st bazooka rocket gun produced (Bridgeport, Connecticut)
French government of Reynaud resigns
First B-29 raid against mainland Japan
Canadian Library Association established
Klemens Gottwald becomes president of Czechoslovakia
WROC TV channel 8 in Rochester, NY (NBC) begins broadcasting
UNIVAC I, the first commercial computer, enters service at the Census Bureau
General strike in Tunisia
Keel is laid for the first nuclear-powered submarine, USS Nautilus (fourth to be named Nautilus)
Military coup by General Gustavo Rojas Pinilla in Colombia
Yanks sweep Indians 6-2, 3-0 before 74,708 win streak at 18 straight
PresidentEisenhowersigns order adding words "under God" to the Pledge
Chile becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
42.0 cm rain falls on East St Louis, Illinois (state record)
British parachutists lands on Cyprus
106°F, hottest temperature in San Francisco
Albert DeSalvo, better known as the Boston Strangler, murders Anna Slesers, his first victim
The European Space Research Organisation is established in Paris - later becoming the European Space Agency
Russian cosmonaut Valery Bykovsky, aboard Vostok 5, orbits earth 81 times in 5 days
Beatles release album "Beatles VI"
Cincinnati Reds pitcher Jim Maloney no-hits New York Mets but loses in 11 innings, 1-0
Dutch police beat construction workers, 60 injured
Miami beats St. Petersburg (Florida State League) 4-3 in 29 innings, the longest uninterrupted game in organized baseball
NASA launches Mariner 5 for a Venus flyby, from Cape Canaveral Air Force Station in Florida
USSR launches Kosmos 166 for observation of Sun from Earth orbit
"In-A-Gadda-Da-Vida" second studio album by Iron Butterfly is released
Off duty Dutch military permitted to wear regular clothing

Members of the NI Social Democratic and Labour Party hold a meeting with representatives of the Irish Republican Army in Derry; the IRA representatives outline their conditions for talks with the British Government
Tropical storm (later hurricane) Agnes forms over Yucatán Peninsula
46th National Spelling Bee: Barrie Trinkle wins spelling vouchsafe
Janis Ian releases "At Seventeen"
USSR launches Venera 10 for Venus landing
"Gong Show" premieres on TV (syndication)
Sierra Leone adopts constitution
Rock group "Little Feat" disbands
No Nukes concert at Hollywood Bowl
Southern Baptist convention decide against allowing women clergy members
"Michael Nesmith In Television Parts" premieres on NBC-TV
Lebanese Shiite Muslim extremists hijacked TWA Flight 847
4th full-duration test firing of redesigned SRB motor
"Guy" debut album by Guy is released
Groundbreaking begins in Bloomington, Minnesota on world's largest mall - "The Mall of America"
MLB Texas Rangers'Nolan Ryanbecomes 2nd pitcher to defeat all 26 teams
Ronald Reaganis knighted by Queen Elizabeth II (honorary knighthood)
Date of the events in the movie Mr Destiny
NL announces plans to expand from 12 to 14 teams for 1993 season
Supreme Court rules police check for drunk drivers constitutional
Leroy Burrell of USA sets 100m record (9.90) in NYC
Space Shuttle STS 40 (Columbia 12) lands
Mona Van Duyn is named 1st female US poet laureate
Japanese space probe Sakigake passes Earth
Tansu Ciller appointed 1st female Prime Minister of Turkey, succeeding Süleyman Demirel
Stanley Cup Final, Madison Square Garden: New York Rangers beat Vancouver Canucks, 3-2 for a 4-3 series victory; Rangers end their record 54-year Championship drought
Giants infielder Mike Benjamin goes 6-for-7 in 13-inning 4-3 win
Karl Krikken out handled the ball for Derbyshire v Indians
"Comic Relief" benefit comedy show
Atlantic, Iowa rainfall of 13.18 inches (33.5 cm) sets new state record
China, Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan form the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
Asafa Powell of Jamaica sets a new Men's 100 meters world record of 9.77 at the Athens Olympic Stadium
Ringo Starr's ninth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include: Billy Squier, Richard Marx, Edgar Winter, Rod Argent, Hamish Stuart, and Sheila E.
An explosion at an Indian steel plant kills 11 people and severely injures 16
Ringo Starr's twelfth All-Starr Band debuts in concert; members include: Steve Lukather, Gregg Rolie, Todd Rundgren, Richard Page, Gregg Bissonette, and Mark Rivera, 2012-13 / Warren Ham, 2014-17
The world's first stem-cell assisted vein transplant is undertaken by Swedish doctors on a 10-year-old girl
Massive flooding occurs in northern India killing up to 10,000 people
49 people are killed after a Ukrainian Ilyushin Il-76 airlifter is shot down
Alexander Stubb becomes Prime Minister of Finland for the first time
"Jurassic World" is the first film to make $500 million worldwide in its opening weekend
First mammal made extinct by human-induced climate change announced - the Bramble Cay melomys from Torres Strait
Bernie Sanders supporter opens fire at Republican politicians practising baseball near Washington, D.C., injuring 4
Fire in Grenfell Tower block in London, England kills 79 and injures 37
Leo Varadkar formally elected Irish Prime Minister (taoiseach) in Dáil ceremony in Dublin - youngest Irish Prime Minister at 38 and 1st openly gay
US Library of Congress names Tracey K. Smith as Poet Laureate
US Senate approves new sanctions against Russia as punishment for meddling in the 2016 election
Malaysia's national debt reassessed at $250 billion, up from previous estimate $170 billion, 80% of gross domestic product
US government confirms 1500 boys being held separated from their parents in Casa Padre, shelter facility for illegal immigrants in a former Walmart in Brownsville, Texas
Petition to create the world's first "time-free zone" on Sommarøy Island, which experiences complete daylight for two months, is delivered to the Norwegian Parliament
Swiss women hold a national strike over the country's slow pace towards equality
India reports surge of nearly 12,000 a day confirmed COVID-19 cases (320,922 overall), the world's fourth-affected country, as death toll hits 9,195
Heatwave event begins across western US and Canadian states with Denver at 101 degrees and Helena 105 degrees
Denmark and Canada agree to split the arctic Hans Island, ending their 50-year "Whiskey War", where each country laid claim by buying whiskey on the island
First controversial UK flight to take asylum seekers to Rwanda cancelled after last-minute legal ruling from European Court of Human Rights of "real risk of irreversible harm"
Last part of the world's largest floating restaurant, the Jumbo Kingdom seafood restaurant, leaves Hong Kong after 46 years and sinks four days later near the Paracel Islands
An overcrowded fishing boast carrying refugees and migrants capsizes south of Greece killing at least 79, with 104 rescued but hundreds more feared dead
Fossil bones found in Tam Pà Ling (Cave of the Monkeys), Laos, dated to 86,000 years, are oldest known examples of modern humans (Homo sapiens) in South-East Asia

Phosphates discovered on Enceladus, a moon of Saturn. Last of essential building blocks of life found there (others carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen and sulfur) raising possibility of extraterrestrial life
‘Benchmark’ experiment by IBM quantum computer manages to simulate a magnetic material, working round previous problem of quantum noise
Series of 'No Kings' protests occur in all 50 states across the United States, opposing the Trump administration on the same day as the United States Army military parade in Washington, D.C.
Henry the Young King, is crowned aged 15 at Westminster Abbey, during the lifetime of his father Henry II in an attempt to settle the succession
Battle of Naseby, Leicestershire: Parliament's New Model Army underOliver CromwellandThomas Fairfaxdefeats the Royalist forces of English KingCharles I
English KingWilliam III's army lands at Carrickfergus in Ireland
US Army first forms as the Continental Army to fight American Revolutionary War
US Continental Congress adopts the Stars & Stripes flag, designed byFrancis Hopkinson, replacing the Grand Union flag
Napoleonforms Ligurian Republic
Charles Babbageproposes a "difference engine" in a paper to the Royal Astronomical Society entitled "Note on the application of machinery to the computation of astronomical and mathematical tables"
Robert Bunseninvents the Bunsen burner
Confederates burn the B&O Railroad bridge and the US Armory buildings at Harpers Ferry before evacuating in face of GeneralGeorge McClellan's advance
Henry Ossian Flipperbecomes 1st African American to graduate from West Point Military Academy
Funeral forEmily Davisonincludes a procession of 6,000 suffragettes in London, England
The South African Government pass the Immigration Act, which restricts the entry and free movement of Asians; it leads to widespread agitation and rioting by resident Indians, led byGandhi
Democratic Convention convenes in St Louis;Woodrow Wilsoncampaigns on the slogan "he kept out of the war"
US PresidentWarren G. Hardingis 1st US President to use radio, dedicates the Francis Scott Key memorial in Baltimore
Republican National Convention, meeting in Kansas City, nominatesHerbert Hooverfor President
Future Head of the Nazi Gestapo,Reinhard Heydrichfirst meets withHeinrich Himmler, architect of the Holocaust
German government ofFranz von Papenforms
MLB'sLou GehrigandJoe McCarthythrown out of game, McCarthy suspended 3 games but Gehrig isn't, so he continues his streak at 1,249 games
American boxerMax BaerKO's defending championPrimo Carneraof Italy in 11th round at Madison Square Garden for the lineal world heavyweight title
European despotsAdolf Hitler, of Germany, andBenito Mussolini, of Italy, meet in Vienna, Austria

Auschwitzconcentration and extermination camp opens in Nazi controlled Poland with Polish POWs, later expanded to include civilian Jews, Roman Catholics, Gypsies and Soviet POWs (at least 1.1 million would die within its walls)
Ground broken forBoeing PlantII (ex-AFLC Plant 13) in Wichita, Kansas
Anne Frankbegins writing her diary, in Dutch, two days after her 13th birthday
French GeneralCharles de Gaullereturns to France, landing in Normandy, proclaiming Bayeux the capital of Free France
Nat King Colerecords "The Christmas Song" (written by Mel Tormé and Bob Wells) for the first time
French-allied State of Vietnam is officially formed during the First Indochina War,Bảo Đạiinstalled as Emperor
Boston BraveWarren Spahnties NL record held by Jim Whitney with 18 strikeouts against the Cubs in 15 innings
British runnerJim Petersruns world record marathon (2:20:42.2)
Eisenhowercondemns McCarthy's book burning proposal
Elvis Presleygraduates from L. C. Humes High School in Memphis, Tennessee
Edouard Carpentier beatsLou Theszto become NWA wrestling champion
NY MetDuke Sniderhits his 400th HR
DrummerRingo Starrre-unites with the Beatles in Melbourne, Australia, after recovering from tonsillitis
John Lennon's second book "A Spaniard in the Works" is published
"TheSteve AllenComedy Hour" premieres on CBS-TV
British film "To Sir, with Love" written and directed byJames Clavell, and starringSidney Poitieris 1st released in the US
California GovernorRonald Reagansigns the Therapeutic Abortion Act, legalizing abortions in the state under certain circumstances, the second state after Colorado to do so
John LennonandYoko Onoappear onDavid Frost's British TV Show
Oakland As'Reggie Jacksongets 10 RBIs to beat Red Sox 21-7
US PresidentRichard Nixonadministration imposes 60-day economy-wide price freeze, superseding Special Rule No. 1 for oil companies
MLB Angels'Nolan Ryanstrikes out 19 Boston Red Sox over 13 innings in a 15-inning 2-1 win for California at Anaheim Stadium
NY GiantsWillie McCovey513th HR is an NL lefty record
"Theme From New York, New York" byFrank Sinatraspreads to #32 on the charts
Earl Weavercomes out of retirement to manage Baltimore Orioles
Woman suesChuck Berryfor $5,000,000, alleges he hit her
"You've Got A Friend" singer-songwriterCarole Kinggets a star on Hollywood's Walk of Fame
Zsa Zsa Gaborarrested for slapping Beverly Hills motorcycle patrolman
"Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves" opens directed by Kevin Reynolds and starringKevin CostnerandMorgan Freeman
MLB St. Louis Cardinals shortstopOzzie Smithbreaks Roy McMillan's NL mark by taking part in his 1,305th career double play
Ruth Bader Ginsburgis introduced by PresidentBill Clintonas his nominee  to the United States Supreme Court in a Rose Garden ceremony at the White House
"Cable Guy" starringJim Carreyis released
Greg Madduxmakes his 387th putout, breakingJack Morris' career record
"The Bourne Identity" directed by Doug Liman and starringMatt Damonis released in the US
Hassan Rouhaniis elected President of Iran
The US government charges NSA leakerEdward Snowdenwith violating the Espionage Act and theft of government property
21st FIFA World Cup opens at the Luzhniki Stadium in Moscow, Russia with British singerRobbie Williamsand Russian soprano Aida Garifullina performing
Rashid Khanmakes his Test debut for Afghanistan against India
US Justice Department concludesJames Comeywas "insubordinate" in his handling of Hilliary Clinton investigation in 2016
Columbia Records releasesBruce Springsteen's 19th studio album "Western Stars"
Cristiano Ronaldoremoves sponsors coke bottles from his press table at the European Championships, prompting the company share price to drop $4 billion
K-pop groupBTSannounce they are taking a break to pursue individual projects
Richard IIin England meets leaders of the Peasants' Revolt on Blackheath. The Tower of London is stormed by rebels who enter without resistance.
CaptainWilliam Blighand his loyal men cast off from HMS Bounty reach Timor, after sailing 5,800 km in a 6-metre launch
Argentina surrenders to the United Kingdom, ending the 74-day Falklands Islands conflict
